dailyJudgementSnippet: Google Gemini had the best Hacker News vibe today: the Gemma 4 thread was notably upbeat, with commenters praising it as a strong local coding model, while OpenAI was mixed, Anthropic was mostly discussed through Mythos-related cyber-risk worries, and Microsoft Copilot drew clear product frustration [E1][E4][E5][E7].
winnerExplanation: Google Gemini wins because it had the strongest clearly positive coverage plus more than one relevant mention. The Gemma 4 discussion was overtly favorable and practical, not just a launch mention, and Gemini also got a direct success callout in a separate model comparison. OpenAI had some praise but also sharp skepticism about its competitive position; Anthropic and Microsoft Copilot skewed negative overall.

openai.judgementSnippet: Mixed. ChatGPT received a positive anecdotal benchmark mention, but another prominent thread argued OpenAI’s lead and valuation story are weakening and that it lacks durable distribution advantages [E3][E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Negative. Anthropic was mainly discussed via Mythos-related security fears, including comments about urgent banking-sector briefings, and Opus also took a mild hit in a user comparison for needing extra prompting [E5][E6].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Positive. Gemma 4 got the day’s warmest direct model-quality praise, especially for local coding use, and Gemini also got a straightforward 'success' mention in a separate comparison [E1][E2].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: Negative. The Copilot-in-Windows thread read as backlash: commenters mocked the rename/spin, disliked AI features being prioritized over basics, and described Microsoft 365 Copilot as disappointing [E7].

evidence.summary: 'I ran Gemma 4 as a local model in Codex CLI' generated multiple favorable comments about Gemma 4, including that it is an 'outlier in its weight class,' impressive locally, and genuinely useful for coding on Macs.
In the math-paper thread, a top-level user comparison of LLMs explicitly listed 'Gemini - success,' giving Google’s model a small but direct positive quality signal.
The same user comparison said 'ChatGPT(free) - did it from the first try,' which is a direct positive anecdotal performance mention for OpenAI.
In the 'AI loser may end up winning' discussion, a prominent comment argued OpenAI’s original valuation logic is breaking down, competitors have caught up, and OpenAI cannot win on distribution.

The 'insane timeline of hacks' thread included a top comment emphasizing urgent US and Canadian financial-sector briefings about cyber risks from Anthropic’s Mythos, creating a distinctly negative association.
A model-comparison comment said Opus initially failed the math-paper task by calling part of it circular and only succeeded after being told ChatGPT had done it.

The Windows Copilot renaming story drew complaints that Microsoft is prioritizing AI add-ons over basic product improvements, and one commenter said Microsoft 365 Copilot was 'deeply disappointing' on practical document tasks.
